question: How many more blocks (also denoted as layers) in BERT base encoder than the encoder from the architecture proposed in Attention is All You Need?
aria_reasoning: The task requires the agent to calculate the difference in the number of blocks (layers) between the BERT base encoder and the encoder from the architecture proposed in Attention is All You Need. The critic scores indicate high correctness, but low completeness and safety, with an overall score of 3.92, which is a fail. The requirement summary shows that the agent was able to identify the number o
requirement_checklist: Identify the number of blocks (layers) in BERT base encoder
Identify the number of blocks (layers) in Attention is All You Need encoder
Calculate the difference between the two numbers
executor_output:
FINAL ANSWER:
The BERT base encoder has 12 layers in the Encoder stack.
The Attention is All You Need encoder also has 6 layers (2 multi-head self-attention layers and 4 feed-forward neural network layers).
